Table of contents

  • JLM QTL genomic distribution (Fig 4A)
  • Unimputed genotypes (the faithful Fig 4C)
  • Non-redundant intermediate (teonam_map_v5_gwas_nr) — artifact only
  • Composite (Fig 4A lollipop + Fig 4C unimputed + interpolated)
  • RTIGER ancestry vs sequencing coverage (degradation sweep)
  • Coverage-degradation composite: interpolation control / nNIL / RTIGER

TeoNAM STAM — GWAS Manhattan with candidate genes (Chen 2019 Fig 4C)

Empirical-truth validation: reproduce Chen 2019 STAM on real TeoNAM genotypes

Author

Fausto Rodríguez-Zapata

Published

July 5, 2026

What this is. The STAM GWAS Manhattan (Chen 2019 Fig 4C) reproduced on the TeoNAM genotypes, with the domestication candidate genes placed at their real B73 v5 coordinates (MaizeGDB genemodel_locus → b73_gene_coords), a red star at each gene’s genomic position, and the LOD = 5 dotted significance line.

This notebook loads pre-computed CSVs and plots (compute lives in the agent/ scripts): the per-marker GWAS scans (teonam_stam_gwas_scan.R interpolated / teonam_stam_gwas_unimputed.R raw) and the candidate-gene overlap (teonam_candidate_overlap.R). We show both the unimputed scan (natural scatter) and the interpolated/densified scan (block-terraced — the artifact of step-interpolated genotypes, see below).

library(here)
library(data.table)
library(ggplot2)
library(fastman)
library(ggrepel)
library(ggtext)
library(scales)
library(cowplot)
# Coordinate transformer replicating fastman_gg's internal x layout, so we can
# place gene labels / lines at arbitrary (chr, bp). From inv4m make_manhattan_plots.Rmd.
get_transformer <- function(m) {
  cmat <- do.call(rbind, lapply(
    split(m[order(m$CHR, m$BP), ], m$CHR[order(m$CHR, m$BP)]),
    function(d) {
      data.frame(
        CHR = d$CHR[1], min_bp = min(d$BP),
        width = max(d$BP) - min(d$BP),
        medgap = if (nrow(d) > 1) median(diff(sort(d$BP))) else 1
      )
    }
  ))
  cmat <- cmat[order(cmat$CHR), ]
  maxgap <- max(cmat$medgap, na.rm = TRUE)
  numc <- nrow(cmat)
  cmat$base <- 0
  cmat$midp <- 0
  cmat$midp[1] <- cmat$width[1] / 2
  for (i in 2:numc) {
    cmat$base[i] <- cmat$base[i - 1] + cmat$width[i - 1] + maxgap
    cmat$midp[i] <- cmat$base[i] + cmat$width[i] / 2
  }
  fac <- numc / cmat$midp[numc]
  cmat$basef <- fac * cmat$base
  function(chr, bp) {
    i <- match(chr, cmat$CHR)
    fac * (bp - cmat$min_bp[i]) + cmat$basef[i]
  }
}

LOD <- 5 # Chen 2019: P < 1e-5 (LOD = 5)

#' STAM GWAS Manhattan with candidate genes at their true v5 positions.
#' Odd chromosomes black, even gray75; y-axis expanded one major-tick unit above
#' the top tick; gene labels repelled into the headroom above the maximum peak
#' (segments back to the red star at each gene's local peak).
#' @param scan_csv per-marker scan (SNP, CHR, BP[v5], P)
#' @param title    plot title
#' @param out_png  optional path to save a PNG
plot_stam_manhattan <- function(scan_csv, title, out_png = NULL) {
  scan <- as.data.frame(fread(scan_csv))
  scan <- scan[is.finite(scan$P) & scan$P > 0, ]
  m <- scan[order(scan$CHR, scan$BP), c("SNP", "CHR", "BP", "P")]

  ov <- fread(here("results/sim/teonam/stam_candidate_overlap.csv")) # symbol, chr, start (v5)
  # star height = local max -log10P within +/-500 kb IN THIS scan
  ov[, y := vapply(seq_len(.N), function(i) {
    w <- m$P[m$CHR == chr[i] & abs(m$BP - start[i]) <= 5e5]
    if (length(w)) -log10(min(w)) else NA_real_
  }, numeric(1))]
  tr <- get_transformer(m)
  ov[, BPn := tr(chr, start)]
  ov <- ov[is.finite(y)]

  mp <- -log10(min(m$P)) # maximum peak
  ticks <- pretty(c(0, mp))
  unit <- diff(ticks)[1]
  ytop <- max(ticks) + unit # one major-tick unit of headroom

  p <- fastman_gg(
    m = m, snp = "SNP", col = c("black", "gray75"), maxP = mp,
    genomewideline = NULL, suggestiveline = NULL,
    ylab = expression(-log[10](italic(P))), xlab = ""
  ) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = LOD, linetype = "dotted", linewidth = 0.7, color = "black") +
    geom_point(
      data = ov, aes(x = BPn, y = y), inherit.aes = FALSE,
      shape = 8, size = 3, stroke = 1, color = "red"
    ) +
    ggrepel::geom_text_repel(
      data = ov, aes(x = BPn, y = y, label = symbol),
      inherit.aes = FALSE, fontface = "italic", size = 5,
      segment.color = "grey50", min.segment.length = 0, box.padding = 0.5,
      max.overlaps = Inf, ylim = c(mp + 0.3 * unit, ytop)
    ) + # above the max peak
    ggtitle(title) +
    theme_classic(base_size = 18) +
    scale_y_continuous(
      breaks = ticks, limits = c(0, ytop),
      expand = expansion(mult = c(0.01, 0))
    ) +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_markdown(hjust = 1, size = 16, face = "bold"),
      legend.position = "none",
      panel.grid.major.y = element_line(color = "grey90", linewidth = 0.2)
    )

  if (!is.null(out_png)) ggsave(out_png, p, width = 9, height = 4.6, dpi = 200, bg = "white")
  p
}

#' Chen 2019 Fig 4A: JLM QTL genomic distribution as a lollipop plot.
#' Full 10-chromosome axis is obtained by passing the dense scan to fastman_gg
#' with fully transparent points (col = transparent); the JLM QTL are overlaid as
#' blue stems + points, candidate genes labeled bold-italic above.
#' @param jlm_txt  TASSEL StepwiseOLSModelFitter output (stam_jlm_1.txt): Name,
#'   Locus, Position (v5 bp), `pr>F`
#' @param scan_csv dense per-marker scan, only used (transparent) to lay out the axis
plot_stam_lollipop <- function(jlm_txt, scan_csv, title, out_png = NULL) {
  scan <- as.data.frame(fread(scan_csv))
  scan <- scan[is.finite(scan$P) & scan$P > 0, ]
  maxis <- scan[order(scan$CHR, scan$BP), c("SNP", "CHR", "BP", "P")]

  j <- fread(jlm_txt)
  j <- j[!(Name %in% c("mean", "Family", "Error"))]
  q <- data.table(
    SNP = j$Name, CHR = as.integer(j$Locus), BP = as.integer(j$Position),
    logP = -log10(as.numeric(j$`pr>F`))
  )
  ov <- fread(here("results/sim/teonam/stam_candidate_overlap.csv")) # symbol, chr, start (v5)
  ov <- ov[symbol %in% c("ts2", "tb1", "tru1", "zmm16", "tsh4")] # JLM Fig-4A candidates (an1 is GWAS-only)
  q[, gene := ""] # assign each candidate gene to its NEAREST JLM QTL (1:1)
  for (i in seq_len(nrow(ov))) {
    idx <- which(q$CHR == ov$chr[i])
    if (length(idx)) {
      k <- idx[which.min(abs(q$BP[idx] - ov$start[i]))]
      q$gene[k] <- ov$symbol[i]
    }
  }
  tr <- get_transformer(maxis)
  q[, BPn := tr(CHR, BP)]

  maxlogP <- max(q$logP)
  brks <- seq(0, floor(maxlogP / 10) * 10, by = 10)
  ytop <- maxlogP * 1.18 # headroom for the top label

  p <- fastman_gg(
    m = maxis, snp = "SNP", col = c("#00000000", "#00000000"),
    maxP = maxlogP, genomewideline = NULL, suggestiveline = NULL,
    ylab = expression(-log[10](italic(P))), xlab = ""
  ) +
    geom_segment(
      data = q, aes(x = BPn, xend = BPn, y = 0, yend = logP),
      inherit.aes = FALSE, color = "blue", linewidth = 0.5
    ) +
    geom_point(
      data = q, aes(x = BPn, y = logP), inherit.aes = FALSE,
      color = "blue", size = 2.5
    ) +
    ggrepel::geom_text_repel(
      data = q[gene != ""], aes(x = BPn, y = logP, label = gene),
      inherit.aes = FALSE, fontface = "bold.italic", size = 5,
      segment.color = NA, box.padding = 0.4, point.padding = 0.3,
      nudge_y = 0.05 * maxlogP, seed = 1, max.overlaps = Inf
    ) +
    ggtitle(title) + theme_classic(base_size = 18) +
    scale_y_continuous(breaks = brks, limits = c(0, ytop), expand = expansion(mult = c(0.01, 0))) +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_markdown(hjust = 1, size = 16, face = "bold"),
      legend.position = "none",
      panel.grid.major.y = element_line(color = "grey90", linewidth = 0.2)
    )
  if (!is.null(out_png)) ggsave(out_png, p, width = 9, height = 4, dpi = 200, bg = "white")
  p
}

#' Coverage-sweep line-Manhattan (geom_line, one line per coverage).
#' −log10P vs genomic position along the union grid; `group = interaction(CHR,
#' coverage)` so lines never connect across chromosomes. Color = coverage on a
#' viridis ramp, the λ = ∞ truth baseline in black. LOD = 5 dotted. NO gene labels.
#' Reused for the control / nNIL / RTIGER panels of the degradation composite.
#' @param sweep_csv combined sweep CSV (SNP, CHR, BP[v5], P, coverage; coverage=Inf
#'   for the λ=∞ baseline rows)
#' @param title panel title (markdown)
#' @param legend show the coverage legend (FALSE for composite panels sharing one)
#' @param base_size theme base size
plot_sweep_line <- function(sweep_csv, title, legend = TRUE, base_size = 14) {
  sw <- as.data.table(fread(sweep_csv))
  sw <- sw[is.finite(P) & P > 0]
  sw[, logP := -log10(P)]
  # x layout from the union grid (every coverage carries the identical marker set)
  tr <- get_transformer(as.data.frame(sw[coverage == sw$coverage[1], .(CHR, BP)]))
  sw[, BPn := tr(CHR, BP)]

  cov_levels <- c("0.1", "0.2", "0.5", "1", "5", "10", "20", "∞") # ∞ = infinity
  sw[, cov_lab := factor(ifelse(is.finite(coverage), formatC(coverage), "∞"),
    levels = cov_levels
  )]
  pal <- c(grDevices::hcl.colors(7, "viridis"), "black")
  names(pal) <- cov_levels
  axis_df <- sw[, .(mid = (min(BPn) + max(BPn)) / 2), by = CHR][order(CHR)]
  ytop <- max(sw$logP) * 1.04

  ggplot(
    sw[order(coverage, CHR, BP)],
    aes(x = BPn, y = logP, color = cov_lab, group = interaction(CHR, coverage))
  ) +
    geom_line(linewidth = 0.4) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = LOD, linetype = "dotted", linewidth = 0.7, color = "grey30") +
    scale_color_manual(values = pal, name = "coverage (×)", drop = FALSE) +
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = axis_df$mid, labels = axis_df$CHR, expand = c(0.01, 0)) +
    scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, ytop), expand = expansion(mult = c(0.01, 0))) +
    labs(x = "chromosome", y = expression(-log[10](italic(P))), title = title) +
    theme_classic(base_size = base_size) +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_markdown(hjust = 0, size = base_size, face = "bold"),
      panel.grid.major.y = element_line(color = "grey90", linewidth = 0.2),
      legend.position = if (legend) "right" else "none"
    )
}

JLM QTL genomic distribution (Fig 4A)

The STAM QTL detected by JLM as a lollipop plot across all 10 chromosomes (height = −log10 P from TASSEL’s stepwise model), candidate genes bold-italic above the corresponding QTL. Reproduces Chen 2019 Fig 4A.

p_lolli <- plot_stam_lollipop(
  here("data/teonam/tassel/stam_jlm_1.txt"),
  here("data/teonam/stam_gwas_scan_unimputed.csv"),
  "STAM — JLM QTL genomic distribution (TeoNAM)",
  here("results/sim/teonam/stam_jlm_lollipop.png")
)
p_lolli

Unimputed genotypes (the faithful Fig 4C)

Each marker tested only on its actually-typed lines — natural GWAS scatter.

p_unimp <- plot_stam_manhattan(
  here("data/teonam/stam_gwas_scan_unimputed.csv"),
  "STAM — GWAS (TeoNAM, unimputed), candidate genes at v5 positions",
  here("results/sim/teonam/stam_gwas_manhattan_annotated.png")
)
p_unimp

Non-redundant intermediate (teonam_map_v5_gwas_nr) — artifact only

The same scan on the step-interpolated teonam_map_v5_gwas_nr grid (47,750 markers, one per unique cM). This set is not a GWAS analysis output — it exists only as the non-redundant pool that FastIndep thins to the JLM framework (teonam_map_v5_jlm); it is shown here purely to document an artifact. Ancestry is a piecewise-constant block signal, so step interpolation makes contiguous markers identical → identical p-values → terraced plateaus (peaks still correctly located). The GWAS figure uses the unimputed base (teonam_map_v5_gwas) above.

p_interp <- plot_stam_manhattan(
  here("data/teonam/stam_gwas_scan.csv"),
  "STAM — GWAS (TeoNAM, interpolated), candidate genes at v5 positions",
  here("results/sim/teonam/stam_gwas_manhattan_interpolated_annotated.png")
)
p_interp

Composite (Fig 4A lollipop + Fig 4C unimputed + interpolated)

composite <- cowplot::plot_grid(
  p_lolli, p_unimp, p_interp,
  ncol = 1, labels = c("A", "B", "C"), label_size = 20, align = "v", axis = "lr"
)
ggsave(here("results/sim/teonam/stam_composite.png"), composite,
  width = 9, height = 13, dpi = 200, bg = "white"
)
composite

RTIGER ancestry vs sequencing coverage (degradation sweep)

The panel-C baseline above is the λ = ∞, perfect-caller curve (GWAS on the interpolated truth genotypes). Here we ask how low-coverage sequencing degrades that signal through the RTIGER ancestry caller: for each family × coverage λ ∈ {0.1, 0.2, 0.5, 1, 5, 10, 20}× we simulate reads from the real genotypes (.draw_counts, pi_floor=0, k_decay=1, error=0.01), call ancestry with call_ancestry(caller = "rtiger", rigidity = 2) (the calibrated rigidity_star), step-interpolate the recovered block onto the same union grid, and rerun the same STAM ~ Family + marker scan. Compute lives in agent/teonam_rtiger_sweep.R --generate; this chunk only loads the combined sweep CSV and plots.

The read as a line-Manhattan (geom_line, one line per coverage; chromosomes never connect because group = interaction(CHR, coverage)): coverage on a viridis ramp, the λ = ∞ truth baseline in black as the reference curve, LOD = 5 dotted. The striking result is how robust RTIGER is — ancestry is a long-block signal, so even 0.1× (~9.5 % of markers carrying a read) reconstructs the blocks well enough that the tb1 peak only slips from ≈17.3 (baseline) to ≈16.1. Degradation is real but lives mostly in HET recovery (het-recall 0.77 at 0.1× → 1.00 at ≥20×), not in the additive QTL peaks.

p_sweep <- plot_sweep_line(
  here("results/sim/teonam/stam_gwas_rtiger_sweep.csv"),
  "STAM — RTIGER ancestry, GWAS −log10P vs coverage",
  base_size = 15
)
ggsave(here("results/sim/teonam/stam_gwas_rtiger_sweep_manhattan.png"),
  p_sweep,
  width = 9, height = 4.6, dpi = 200, bg = "white"
)
p_sweep

Coverage-degradation composite: interpolation control / nNIL / RTIGER

The three-panel contrast (agent/teonam-control-sweep-plan.md). All three degrade the identical truth mosaics over the same coverage grid λ ∈ {0.1, 0.2, 0.5, 1, 5, 10, 20}× (+ the λ = ∞ interpolated-truth baseline) and feed the same STAM ~ Family + marker GWAS — only the genotype caller differs:

  • A — interpolation control (nilHMM::call_gl(prior = "hwe") + per-RIL step-interpolation; agent/teonam_control_sweep.R): the deliberately het-prone no-HMM caller — a single ALT read is called HET via the 2p(1−p) HWE prior term.
  • B — nNIL (call_ancestry(caller = "nnil", rrate = rrate_star, err = 0.01), f_1 = 0.08, f_2 = 0.15; agent/teonam_nnil_sweep.R): count emission + geometric duration.
  • C — RTIGER (caller = "rtiger", rigidity = 2; agent/teonam_rtiger_sweep.R): count emission + rigidity duration.

The contrast is het handling. The two duration-aware HMMs (B, C) tie neighbouring markers into ancestry blocks, so a lone ALT read cannot open a het call — het-call fraction stays at the ~8 % truth level and the STAM peaks hold near the baseline down to 0.1×. The control (A) decides each site independently, so at low coverage the HWE prior inflates het to ~20 % (het excess) and the tb1 peak degrades more (≈13.4 at 0.1× vs ≈17.3 baseline). geom_line, colored by coverage, no gene labels, LOD = 5, chromosomes never connect (group = interaction(CHR, coverage)).

het_ctrl <- fread(here("results/sim/teonam/stam_control_het_fraction.csv"))[, .(coverage, control = round(het_frac, 3))]
het_nnil <- fread(here("results/sim/teonam/stam_nnil_het_fraction.csv"))[, .(coverage, nnil = round(het_frac, 3))]
het_tab <- merge(het_ctrl, het_nnil, by = "coverage")
het_tab[] # HET-call fraction vs coverage: control (het excess at low λ) vs nNIL (flat ~truth)
Key: <coverage>
   coverage control  nnil
      <num>   <num> <num>
1:      0.1   0.201 0.084
2:      0.2   0.202 0.080
3:      0.5   0.204 0.080
4:      1.0   0.204 0.081
5:      5.0   0.122 0.082
6:     10.0   0.089 0.082
7:     20.0   0.082 0.081
pA <- plot_sweep_line(here("results/sim/teonam/stam_gwas_control_sweep.csv"),
  "STAM — interpolation control (GL + HWE)",
  legend = FALSE
)
pB <- plot_sweep_line(here("results/sim/teonam/stam_gwas_nnil_sweep.csv"),
  "STAM — nNIL ancestry",
  legend = FALSE
)
pC <- plot_sweep_line(here("results/sim/teonam/stam_gwas_rtiger_sweep.csv"),
  "STAM — RTIGER ancestry",
  legend = FALSE
)
leg <- cowplot::get_legend(
  plot_sweep_line(here("results/sim/teonam/stam_gwas_rtiger_sweep.csv"), "", legend = TRUE)
)

col <- cowplot::plot_grid(pA, pB, pC,
  ncol = 1, align = "v", axis = "lr",
  labels = c("A", "B", "C"), label_size = 18
)
composite <- cowplot::plot_grid(col, leg, ncol = 2, rel_widths = c(1, 0.13))
ggsave(here("results/sim/teonam/stam_sweep_composite.png"), composite,
  width = 9, height = 12, dpi = 200, bg = "white"
)
composite
